textByHash.38b3a88107932342f73a1c47580b3a582c4db14f: Perhaps. But I still believe the vyrewatch can handle this.
textByHash.23d2121c62559ceb368a5bdac234c2c38d173d6d: I may have a solution to that, if this is indeed the route we're taking. There is also the question of your sister. She's specifically requested that Safalaan and that outsider be left to her.
textByHash.cb0a3eddd17b5be3344c28e7eaa7df88c2003fe3: Very well, I will make preparations immediately. The sooner we deal with the Myreque the better. Even our kind have weaknesses and if Serafina could discover them, the Myreque can as well.
textByHash.0c158fb9685673db40141e105d9eebed0fbed059: Hmmm... Lord Drakan won't be pleased about this.
textByHash.1244937e5befa942a8cc71d5ce2ae3fefe40e3bb: So yes, Lady Vanescula, we're getting maximum yield from the current rotation. And more tithes are coming in all the time.
textByHash.05ef426f95a58b642284d308d1d1ba776ba02b76: If I remember, madam, I had little choice. You were quite... 'persuasive' the last time we met.
textByHash.cf8eda66e53388ddd86596dfc266db542ebaa93e: Perhaps a light refreshment m'lady? This is from the fresh batch.
textByHash.d04d88984408c22bdab596de70c721b8eb80ffd8: The same is true for me, m'lady.
textByHash.e841f2bef25970b4dc904895540159e05d4b6632: Ha! So we meet again. Prepare to die!
textByHash.a10f98471b2f13546d015243a6aeed8d3dc1a6b4: I take no orders from food sacks like you, Guile. Attend to this yourself. In any case, he wasn't much of a punch bag anyway.
textByHash.92fb989bfbcbb099754a0bdafe5e7c3cb1f6117b: I take no orders from food sacks like you, Guile. Attend to this yourself. In any case, she wasn't much of a punch bag anyway.
textByHash.e3a514f021ff005af42eb1a1ce68cfc570fb5a4d: I saw your wife today, Gadderanks. She's well.
textByHash.c66c3a0f9fbdb4eac2303040f0ffb47363787b20: I hope she stays that way.
textByHash.2bd498071ffb86f33f51dec30d4b6e459ddeab1e: I couldn't care less for your problems Gadderanks. Just do the job you've been appointed to do.
textByHash.4befd25a5ae8b7800363585a5a135b869671c55e: Wait... Do you smell that? Smoke. It's coming from Burgh de Rott.
textByHash.4d2a7aae04c5fccfa68840ffd7742a1a7f9e4ac7: No. Burgh de Rott was abandoned. That is clearly no longer the case.
textByHash.767fae0447764209b0784cd821bc7d801dbc7f39: It seems you have a fresh appointment, Gadderanks. I hope there will be no more delay.
textByHash.41d085cfed81e0934d5ca0851a163b6de87564e3: Hello there. The name's Vanstrom Klause. How goes it stranger?
textByHash.7afdb24e35a830550b11db4bb9f45b0fd4e50b57: Hmm, well, I am a little concerned about some friends of mine. They're in dire need of some assistance, but I'm at a loss as to how I can help them.
textByHash.85d754a568c6b73441993b679ed75b75b40ef191: It's a personal tragedy that I have yet to meet them in the flesh. But their exploits make mouth watering stories... The real meat and drink of high adventure and daring... So they say.
textByHash.a0ea93d6e926bd4e41b97eae5a2d5cdf0e01e7e1: They're a group of rebels fighting against the vampyres that rule over Morytania. They're known as the Myreque. Some people call them terrorists while others call them freedom fighters!
textByHash.7e3ba41349cdd1201bd24483fb8304bfedaf001e: Of course. I'm a big fan of their cause. I was hoping to supply them with some new weapons. Alas, I find that I'm rather lacking in ability when it comes to this kind of thing!
textByHash.b332b7ed4f537ced0011c9eec4acbdada9447be2: I was hoping to deliver some steel to them. A longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer.
textByHash.4422be591a9300ae799d9daae5c0e762408ba903: Though if you wish to do this, I'm afraid you'll need to acquire the weapons yourself. My efforts to procure them have been rather unsuccessful.
textByHash.7171846d5efe299c145b38952a78958bcdf2a7e6: The vampyres are always hunting them, so they keep themselves hidden. I hear the boatman in Mort'ton has dealings with them though.
textByHash.93845e5bc20c975054ef04a354597e4c1cc0edb3: How wonderful! Perhaps the people of Morytania now have an additional hero that they can come to rely on?
textByHash.adb2b115bb799f2c78dbed0d8c971667628cb40e: There's always some evil heel ready to grind the face of humanity into the dirt. Here in Morytania, it's the vampyres! They rule over the region with an iron fist, killing any who oppose them.
textByHash.05c8b0c57b901e0e7451a97850f92fd393f62419: The Myreque are the only ones brave enough to stand up to them. They stay in the shadows though, lest the vampyres track them down!
textByHash.3e1d229a7ff57110fc46582082903fb6a84439ab: No one knows for sure how many of them there are. The group hiding in Mort Myre supposedly has six members. There are whispers of more in other parts of Morytania though.
textByHash.8f98a78b2f996a256ba85cdd60392fb610dd92af: Yes, indeed! The Myreque are almost certainly able to handle themselves... But they need the tools to do it!
textByHash.187ff6b27785106f6401240ec3eb328718f438bc: You've forgotten already? Ha! That's funny! But I do understand, my memory isn't what it was either!
textByHash.851025f8cc1720d6973b172af91e9b89ffd1c560: I'd suggest a longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer, all made of steel.
textByHash.ff9a563d2f14954806dc7893caeb57cec908e83d: You'll need to obtain the weapons yourself. Fear not though! If you return to me here once you've found them, I'll be sure to give you a nice surprise in return for your help!
textByHash.7ca68804b6871e50e1214a62adf1b30b5a46ae2b: I hear he lives in Mort'ton. You'll find the town south of here, through Mort Myre. It's a nasty place, but where isn't in Morytania?
textByHash.2c0a8b7576995c4ed92b984eed8dee0abe82e406: Most of the town has supposedly fallen to some strange affliction. The boatman is apparently one of the few healthy people left!
textByHash.d4920a5be50a9324bd79dcb69b326514604f44a6: So I hear. There are many who sympathise with their movement. Rumour is that this boatman's been ferrying supplies to their hideout, somewhere in Mort Myre!
textByHash.d6430ff55312fe620cc31884d1e2c05694448b85: Oh well, I'm sure someone will be along to help soon. Thanks for even considering it though.
textByHash.f0a213b455db856d366b4dfaf36d8ebb02023862: Hello again. Any luck finding the Myreque?
textByHash.4cd19296b3ee7fb634bcbd0c8317273dc4a9466e: Well done adventurer! You led me right to them. The little dears are going to wonder which side you're on!
textByHash.df4eb1f36dd16b05d6b3603d29791e81fe46d9f5: The Drakans have been wanting you lot dealt with for a while now. We wouldn't want to disappoint them, would we?
textByHash.a143aa67a670bf76d6a82332b3b2f3bf485710ad: And now... A little gift for the rest of you.
textByHash.298401ebacb9b1fd2e5e859dec7d4de75c15892e: Alright then, Damien. What is it that you're so keen to show me?
textByHash.863d8937621a9703a218d65a620fa570f0525862: Don't tell me you had me come all the way down here for this? Look at the wretched thing, Damien. It's barely even a proper juvenile.
textByHash.bb8420b6cc238701cfc0eebd70ae9c3bc9d7819f: And just what is this Elixir of Everlasting?
textByHash.00bf02f25309c3e4ef336ee8905b8531875d0c30: So the Sleeper Plague? That was your doing?
textByHash.24738b0bf2330d208631fe7b89b3d4e88fa9db64: And tell me, Damien, who gave you permission to do this?
textByHash.00650957501673580de56e8124d43285aff3a852: That someone is not you. I am in charge of blood tithing. I alone decide how it is to be carried out.
textByHash.920335249ba3945227687a1e5ed930c3310e4a6b: I don't doubt the ingenuity of your plan. This elixir could have great potential. The thing I doubt is your recklessness. This Sleeper Plague has attracted attention. The wrong kind of attention.
textByHash.bcf66f3f5c2d4c1d899ccd6abcf7f96057346438: I do not fear the Myreque. I have no need to. They have no way of touching me.
textByHash.cf0646da549db9b6466cf804a27fb1d7b6dc9aaf: But you on the other hand, you should be very afraid. Out here alone, without the support of the Drakan household. You're a prime target. And if what I hear is true, they're right on your doorstep.
textByHash.ae92de0e9423b38504ef5e5b3bdcf75b64a992b9: The Myreque are already here in Slepe, Damien. The one that killed Ranis Drakan is among them. Your overconfidence has begun your downfall.
textByHash.8b4e82c560ef4480913647379b7b7cefc3fbf9dc: I will do no such thing. You defied Lord Drakan when you came out here. The consequences of your actions are your own to deal with.
textByHash.1191ae10627ab8b3cace1a51c20d364d2a01be79: Now, if you'll excuse me, I will be on my way. There are things in Darkmeyer that require my attention.
textByHash.97a3d00dfa1e6d3e283ce238720649f3dd6d1054: As it so happens I was on my way to the lower city to look into that very issue. However, on my way past, I heard shouting.
textByHash.2c958413605dd5870061c3a9205779ae176039dd: You're paranoid, Jovkai. Lord Drakan doesn't care about you.
textByHash.dbbadb53a9739e8d236aa2d026ee0a7a9a4494c4: Careful there, Jovkai. Now tell me, what's happening here?
textByHash.cfa028e341c2e01928a5be1f2e5a25142b294515: Hang on... I know those humans. That's the Gadderanks family. They don't belong to you.
textByHash.7869031c1d7ce219b3357884ec2d7a98a148bd60: That doesn't make them yours. These prisoners belong to Desmodus Lasiurus. You will have them returned to him at once.
textByHash.9bad8b7e7a4acdfba34165eb12db137ae5fd2fdc: You may be a lord, but you still answer to the Drakan family. That means you also answer to me.
textByHash.0674772bd6206c2fcf74284f529098071020ea1a: I do not care what Lasiurus does with them any more than I care about you and your weird tendencies.
textByHash.b71fe5a183cc36f1bfc5d966555252daadb1e43b: Now, we're done here. See that the prisoners are returned, or I'll have this place shut down.
textByHash.f3a2e1dc5addb16aedcd1ebf9ca856f16f92525c: You will return to Lasiurus and tell him that his prisoners are on their way.
textByHash.d393d3e0d1798ed9e35efd6374f37ababf9737fa: Wait... Your voice, vyre. It sounds... familiar.
textByHash.90c0d6baa059762742a1d08bcccba3ab112f5afd: Clearly. Which makes me wonder why I'd know your voice.
textByHash.1165b1b80b5302ef39f4b8b35c8f77a148030e5d: Turn and face me. I want to get a good look at you.
textByHash.3fcb2680345ecc403284f573741f899596d31c2d: Turn, and face me. I will not ask again.
textByHash.65384364ad5fc1ecb7449a4f373b5f87614efb0d: Fine. My business here is done, regardless.
textByHash.1b56e511905e73cf041afaee1a88db6a6bf136a4: I... I'm sorry my lady, but this just seems too good to be true.
textByHash.403b2433cb01156ce9369c7fecd6efdde480da93: I've waited a long time for this day. The day that the Myreque finally die.
textByHash.bb324a156b2635a86f31e1590d55013cc8d798c3: There'll be no hellhounds or abominations this time though. This time...
textByHash.aaead8be0cfadd0075dbc49e1cb8bcd16475dfbb: How long I have wished to end your pitiful life...
textByHash.391f4f10faf114fae4e16a3ccb82be21e56f49be: My pets will feast on your corpse!
textByHash.cf22a5c965d17576534a354d4a51ca11313d4c7e: Ha! Did you think you could beat me that easily?
textByHash.56ec0073b0ebc5c49cd9e11598f0803974507340: See if you can survive this!
textByHash.62d5451db7f8184708f3da5d5ae831c692c6ee89: Stop? And why would I want to do that?
textByHash.83e357b995c0882c42c1d568dc57e04e8d92394c: I remember how weak Ascertes was! The pathetic love he had for his wife, for his children!
textByHash.dba0499429bb03b1b3f8b3d651d19067a6b0b44d: I remember how good it felt! To be uplifted! To be made something more!
textByHash.69a2e46ba5d697eb8673776cd8177a7afb74572c: And I remember the joy when I took your sister's skull and crushed it with my bare hands!
